name: WinPEAP
description: WinPEAP is a WinPE-based workflow to transition devices to Entra Joined and auto-enroll them into Intune via Windows Autopilot. It uses OSDCloud to build a customized WinPE ISO, injects the 4kAutopilotHashUpload.ps1 script and oa3tool-based hardware hash capture, and uploads the Autopilot hash to Intune during WinPE. Automation spans OS deployment, driver injection, hash registration, and enrollment, with support for user-driven Autopilot profiles and VM testing considerations.
author: Beau LaWalt

securityCheck.aiSummary: The script appears to be a legitimate Intune/Autopilot enrollment tool for WinPE. No obfuscation, remote code execution, or exfiltration behavior identified. Key considerations: ensure placeholders for TenantId/AppId/AppSecret are replaced with secure values before use; avoid leaving Beta Graph endpoints in production without evaluation; consider enforcing TLS 1.2 in WinPE contexts and secure storage/rotation of credentials used for token retrieval.
